purpose: Introduce a powerful Blender integration for Ananta as a controlled creative/runtime surface. The Blender addon must remain a thin control surface and bridge: orchestration, policy, approval, audit, retrieval and execution decisions stay in the Ananta Hub. Blender source code, API documentation and addon references must be ingested through codecompass/rag_helper, not loaded ad hoc by the addon or prompt logic.
goal: Deliver a substantial Blender integration that can inspect scenes, collect bounded scene context, submit goals, show plans/tasks/artifacts/approvals, generate and preview Blender action plans, and execute approved Blender operations through Hub-controlled policies and audit trails.
non_goals: Do not let the Blender addon implement its own orchestration, policy, approval, retrieval or agent loop.
Do not execute free-form LLM-generated Python inside Blender without explicit approval and policy checks.
Do not dump full Blender scenes, full Blender source trees or full API documentation into prompts.
Do not start FreeCAD or KiCad integration in this track.
Do not treat generated Blender scripts as trusted code.
success_criteria: Blender addon installs and loads in a supported Blender version.
Addon can configure Ananta endpoint/profile/auth and perform health/capability checks.
Addon can capture bounded scene context with provenance and size limits.
Addon can submit Blender-related goals to the Ananta Hub without local orchestration.
Addon can display tasks, artifacts, approvals and policy-denied states returned by the Hub.
Blender API/source/docs retrieval is performed through codecompass/rag_helper indexes only.
Read-only actions can be performed safely; mutating actions require approval.
Script execution is default-deny unless routed through Hub policy, approval and audit.
Smoke tests verify addon load, context capture, mocked Hub interaction and policy-denied execution.
Release gate can block false Blender runtime claims.
architecture_guardrails: The Blender addon is a client/control surface, not an autonomous agent runtime.
The Hub owns Goal -> Plan -> Task -> Execution -> Verification -> Artifact orchestration.
The Hub owns policy, approval, audit, bounded-context and retrieval decisions.
All Blender operations must be represented as auditable action plans or backend API calls.
All mutating operations require explicit approval unless a narrowly scoped safe policy allows them.
Blender Python execution must be sandboxed or constrained as much as practical and always auditable.
codecompass/rag_helper is the only supported ingestion path for Blender source/docs/reference code.
RAG output must include provenance: source, version/ref, file/path, symbol/section and retrieval reason.
The addon must show degraded states clearly when Hub, auth, capability or policy checks fail.
The addon must never silently include unrelated local files or secrets in context.

milestones.title: Scene context, provenance and bounded payloads
Goal, task, artifact and approval UX

tasks.title: Blender integration scope and threat model define
acceptance_criteria: Scope distinguishes read-only analysis, preview generation, mutating operations and Python script execution.
Threat model covers malicious prompts, unsafe scripts, file access, network access, addon abuse, scene exfiltration and accidental destructive changes.
The document states that FreeCAD and KiCad are out of scope for this track.
The document states that the addon is a thin control surface and does not own orchestration.

tasks.title: Blender capability model define
acceptance_criteria: Capability model covers scene.read, selection.read, preview.render, export.plan, script.plan, script.execute, object.mutate, material.mutate and animation.mutate.
Each capability declares whether it is read-only, mutating, approval-required or default-deny.
Capabilities are returned by Hub health/capability endpoints and displayed by the addon.

tasks.title: Blender API contract define
acceptance_criteria: Schemas define goal submission, scene context submission, action plan response, approval request and action result payloads.
Schemas include provenance, correlation IDs, capability names and safety classification.
Schemas are compatible with Ananta Client API v1 rather than a separate orchestration protocol.

tasks.title: Blender runtime inventory classification add
acceptance_criteria: Runtime inventory includes Blender with an honest status such as planned, foundation_only, runtime_mvp or deferred.
Inventory lists required files, commands and smoke evidence for any runtime_mvp claim.
Release-gate/audit logic can fail false Blender runtime claims.

tasks.title: Blender RAG source profiles define
acceptance_criteria: Profiles define source type, version/ref, allowed paths, include/exclude globs, max file sizes and intended usage.
Profiles require codecompass/rag_helper ingestion and explicitly forbid ad hoc addon-side documentation loading.
Profiles separate Blender API docs, Blender source code and curated addon examples.
Profiles include licensing/provenance notes where relevant.

tasks.title: codecompass/rag_helper Blender indexing config add
acceptance_criteria: Indexing config uses compact outputs and bounded include globs for Python, Markdown, C/C++ headers where supported, and docs.
Config excludes build outputs, generated files, binary assets, caches, vendored dependencies where not needed and irrelevant large directories.
Config preserves provenance metadata needed for audit and explanations.
Config can be run locally with documented commands.

tasks.title: Blender retrieval service integration add
acceptance_criteria: Service queries codecompass/rag_helper outputs for Blender API/source/doc slices.
Service returns bounded results with source, version/ref, file/path, symbol/section and retrieval reason.
Service supports at least script planning, addon API questions, scene analysis and export/render task retrieval intents.
Service refuses to return unbounded full-doc or full-repo context.

tasks.title: Blender RAG provenance and audit markers add
acceptance_criteria: Tasks using Blender references record which RAG sources influenced the plan or script.
Reference trace is visible in task details, artifacts or audit diagnostics.
Trace distinguishes API documentation, Blender source and addon example influence.

tasks.title: Blender addon project skeleton add
acceptance_criteria: Addon has valid Blender metadata and can be installed as a local addon.
Addon registers and unregisters panels/operators cleanly.
Addon code is separated into UI, operators, API client and context capture modules.
No LLM or policy logic is embedded in the addon.

tasks.title: Endpoint profile auth settings add
acceptance_criteria: Addon exposes Ananta endpoint, profile and auth token settings using Blender preferences or scene-safe configuration.
Secrets are not logged, rendered into error messages or included in scene context.
Settings support degraded states for missing endpoint, missing token and invalid token.

tasks.title: Health and capability check implement
acceptance_criteria: Addon can call Hub health and capabilities endpoints.
Addon displays connected, degraded, unauthorized and backend-unreachable states clearly.
Addon displays Blender-specific capabilities and disabled operations.

tasks.title: Blender panel layout implement
acceptance_criteria: Panel includes connection status, goal input, context preview, task/artifact summary and approval indicators.
Panel avoids becoming a browser clone and keeps Blender workflow compact.
Panel has clear warning states for policy-denied or approval-required actions.

tasks.title: Scene context schema define
acceptance_criteria: Schema covers scene metadata, selected objects, object summaries, materials, lights, cameras, collections, modifiers, geometry-node indicators and file metadata.
Schema includes payload size limits, provenance metadata and redaction markers.
Schema distinguishes summary context from optional user-approved detailed context.

tasks.title: Bounded scene context capture implement
acceptance_criteria: Context capture summarizes selected scene/object/material data without dumping full binary assets or unrelated files.
Capture applies size limits and redacts likely secrets or local-sensitive paths where practical.
Capture includes provenance: scene name, object names, selection state, addon version and Blender version.
Capture can run without a live Hub for local preview/testing.

tasks.title: Context preview and confirmation UI add
acceptance_criteria: User can inspect compact context before submitting a goal.
UI shows what will be sent and what is excluded.
Detailed context expansion requires explicit user action where applicable.

tasks.title: Scene context backend validation add
acceptance_criteria: Backend tests validate accepted, oversized, malformed and secret-like context payloads.
Invalid payloads return structured degraded or rejected states.
Validation does not trust addon-side filtering alone.

tasks.title: Submit Blender goal operator add
acceptance_criteria: Operator submits goal text, selected capability and bounded scene context to the Hub.
Operator receives structured task or degraded-state response.
Operator does not create plans locally.

tasks.title: Task and artifact read models display add
acceptance_criteria: Addon can list recent Blender-related tasks returned by the Hub.
Addon can show artifact summaries such as findings, scripts, renders, previews and export plans.
Addon handles empty, malformed, unauthorized and backend-unreachable states.

tasks.title: Approval queue display add
acceptance_criteria: Addon displays approval-required actions with capability, risk, script summary and expected scene/file effects.
Approve/reject calls backend approval APIs only.
Stale, malformed, denied or unauthorized approval attempts are shown clearly.

tasks.title: Policy-denied UX add
acceptance_criteria: Addon clearly distinguishes policy-denied, approval-required, failed, dry-run-only and completed states.
Denied actions are not retried locally or transformed into direct Blender operations.
Policy messages do not leak secrets or excessive backend details.

tasks.title: Blender action plan model implement
acceptance_criteria: Action plan separates explanation, proposed operations, generated script, expected changes, risks, required capability and approval state.
Plan includes RAG provenance when Blender API/source/docs influenced the proposal.
Plan can be rendered as an artifact before execution.

tasks.title: Read-only Blender operations route add
acceptance_criteria: Read-only operations cover scene summary, selected object summary, material summary, camera/light summary and performance hints.
Read-only operations still go through Hub-visible task/artifact paths where practical.
Read-only operations cannot mutate scene or files.

tasks.title: Dry-run script planning add
acceptance_criteria: Hub can produce Blender Python script proposals as artifacts without executing them.
Scripts include declared capabilities, expected effects and safety notes.
Script proposal uses retrieved Blender API context through codecompass/rag_helper where needed.
Generated script is treated as untrusted until approved and policy-checked.

tasks.title: Approval-gated script execution bridge add
acceptance_criteria: Script execution only occurs after Hub policy permits and approval is granted where required.
Bridge records correlation ID, script hash, capability, approval ID and result.
Bridge returns stdout/stderr/errors/result summary as artifact data.
Execution failure leaves scene state understandable and logged where practical.

tasks.title: Mutation operation wrappers add
acceptance_criteria: Common mutation wrappers exist for creating objects, changing materials, adding modifiers, changing cameras/lights and creating simple animation keys.
Wrappers are capability-tagged and approval-aware.
Wrappers do not bypass Hub action plan, approval or audit.

tasks.title: Blender artifact schema define
acceptance_criteria: Artifact schema covers scene findings, script proposals, executed script reports, render previews, export plans and before/after summaries.
Artifacts include provenance, capability, task ID and safety classification.
Artifacts can be shown in Blender addon, CLI/TUI or Web UI without custom unsafe assumptions.

tasks.title: Preview render artifact support add
acceptance_criteria: Addon can request or create a bounded preview render artifact where allowed.
Preview generation avoids unexpected long-running or high-resource renders by default.
Preview artifact records render settings summary and output path/reference.

tasks.title: Export plan artifact support add
acceptance_criteria: Hub can propose export plans for formats such as glTF, FBX, OBJ or STL without executing by default.
Export execution is capability-tagged and approval-aware when it writes files.
Artifact explains settings, target path and expected side effects.

tasks.title: Blender policy rules add
acceptance_criteria: Policy rules classify read-only, mutating, file-writing, network-touching and script-executing operations.
Script execution is default-deny unless explicitly enabled and approved.
File writes outside approved paths are denied.
Policy denial is testable and visible to clients.

tasks.title: Blender audit events add
acceptance_criteria: Audit events record goal, task, capability, context hash, script hash, approval ID, execution result and artifact IDs.
Audit distinguishes read-only plan, approval request, approval decision, execution and policy denial.
Audit output avoids secrets and excessive scene data.

tasks.title: Secret and path redaction hardening add
acceptance_criteria: Likely secrets, tokens, credentials and sensitive local paths are redacted from context, logs and rendered errors where practical.
Tests include positive and negative redaction cases.
Redaction does not replace policy enforcement.

tasks.title: Resource and timeout controls add
acceptance_criteria: Long-running render/export/script operations have explicit timeouts or user-visible progress states where practical.
Addon and bridge handle cancellation or failure gracefully.
Heavy operations cannot be triggered silently by mere context capture.

tasks.title: Addon import and registration tests add
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies addon modules import without side effects outside Blender where practical.
Headless Blender smoke verifies addon registration if Blender is available.
If Blender binary is unavailable in CI, test is marked manual/advisory and runtime_complete is blocked.

tasks.title: Scene context contract tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover empty scene, selected object, many objects, materials, lights/cameras and oversized payloads.
Tests verify provenance and redaction markers.
Tests verify unrelated files are not silently included.

tasks.title: Mocked Hub interaction tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover health, capabilities, goal submit, task/artifact read, approval-required and policy-denied responses.
Tests verify degraded states render as degraded rather than success.
Tests verify addon does not locally execute denied or approval-required actions.

tasks.title: Policy and approval negative tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover denied script execution, denied file write, stale approval, malformed approval and unauthorized approval.
Tests verify denied actions produce audit/policy artifacts where appropriate.
Tests verify no fallback path bypasses Hub policy.

tasks.title: Blender release gate integration add
acceptance_criteria: Release gate includes Blender inventory/audit checks when Blender runtime_mvp is claimed.
Gate separates automated checks from manual/advisory Blender checks.
Gate fails on skeleton-only or docs-only Blender runtime claims.

tasks.title: Blender integration docs write
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain installation, endpoint setup, health check, scene context preview, goal submission, approval and artifact viewing.
Docs clearly state safety model and limitations.
Docs include tested commands or manual smoke checklist.

tasks.title: Blender golden path examples add
acceptance_criteria: Examples cover scene review, material improvement plan, geometry-node/script plan, preview render and approval-gated execution.
Examples are reproducible with mocked Hub or documented local setup.
Examples avoid claiming unsafe autonomous scene modification.

tasks.title: FreeCAD and KiCad roadmap isolate
acceptance_criteria: Roadmap notes that FreeCAD and KiCad require stricter correctness gates than Blender.
Roadmap keeps FreeCAD/KiCad out of the Blender MVP and this track's release gate.
Roadmap suggests separate future todo files for CAD/EDA integrations.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Define scope, threat model, capabilities, contracts and inventory.
Stage 2: Add Blender RAG ingestion profiles and retrieval via codecompass/rag_helper.
Stage 3: Build addon foundation with endpoint/auth, health and panel layout.
Stage 4: Implement bounded scene context capture, preview and backend validation.
Stage 5: Add goal submission, task/artifact display, approval queue and policy-denied UX.
Stage 6: Add action planning, dry-run script proposals and approval-gated execution bridge.
Stage 7: Add artifacts, previews and export planning.
Stage 8: Harden policy, audit, redaction, timeouts and resource controls.
Stage 9: Add tests, smoke checks and release-gate integration.
Stage 10: Write docs, examples and isolate FreeCAD/KiCad roadmap.
summary_notes: This is intentionally a large Blender track, not a small read-only adapter.
The addon is still a thin Ananta client/control surface; the Hub remains the authority for orchestration, policy, approval, audit and retrieval.
Blender source code and documentation must be ingested through codecompass/rag_helper profiles and queried through bounded retrieval services.
FreeCAD and KiCad are explicitly deferred because their correctness requirements are stricter and should be separate tracks.
This track should start only after core OSS runtime tests and release-gates are sufficiently stable, unless explicitly isolated on its own feature branch.
Consistency sync for blender track performed on 2026-04-30 (milestones, tasks_status_summary, todos fallback).
All blender track tasks completed and retained as done on 2026-04-30.
